starry_unigraph.core.data_split module

Shared train/val/test split utilities.

Provides deterministic, ratio-based splitting of ordered sequences (snapshots or event streams) into train / val / test ranges. Used by both DTDG (FlareRuntimeLoader) and CTDG (TGTemporalDataset) pipelines so the split logic is defined in exactly one place.

starry_unigraph.core.data_split.normalize_split_ratio(split_ratio: dict[str, float] | None) → dict[str, float][source]

Normalize a split ratio dict so its values sum to 1.0.

Args:
split_ratio: Dictionary with keys "train", "val", "test"

mapping to non-negative floats. None or missing keys use defaults (0.7 / 0.15 / 0.15).

Returns:

A new dict whose three values sum to exactly 1.0.

Example:

>>> normalize_split_ratio({"train": 8, "val": 1, "test": 1})
{'train': 0.8, 'val': 0.1, 'test': 0.1}
>>> normalize_split_ratio(None)
{'train': 0.7, 'val': 0.15, 'test': 0.15}
starry_unigraph.core.data_split.split_bounds(total: int, split: str, split_ratio: dict[str, float]) → tuple[int, int][source]

Return (start, end) index range for the requested split.

The sequence [0, total) is divided contiguously into three consecutive segments: train [0, t), val [t, v), test [v, total). Rounding remainders are given to the splits with the largest fractional parts, and every split with a positive ratio is guaranteed at least one element when total >= 3.

Args:

total: Length of the full sequence (number of snapshots or events). split: One of "train", "val", or "test". split_ratio: Normalized ratio dict (values sum to 1.0), typically

produced by normalize_split_ratio().

Returns:

(start, end) — a half-open index range suitable for range(start, end) or slice [start:end].

Raises:

KeyError: If split is not "train", "val", or "test".

Example:

>>> ratio = normalize_split_ratio({"train": 0.7, "val": 0.15, "test": 0.15})
>>> split_bounds(200, "train", ratio)
(0, 140)
>>> split_bounds(200, "val", ratio)
(140, 170)
>>> split_bounds(200, "test", ratio)
(170, 200)
